feat: v2.5.0 Week 2 - IncrementalCompressor 集成 + lobster_grep 重排序 (#92)

## 改动内容

### IncrementalCompressor 集成（v2.5.0）
- 在 __init__() 中初始化 TFIDFScorer 和 SemanticDeduplicator
- on_new_message() 调用 score_and_tag() 评分和打标签
- 将评分信息（msg_type, tfidf_score, compression_exempt）添加到消息
- compress() 实现三层压缩策略：
  - <60% 不压缩
  - 60-75% 仅去重
  - >75% DAG 压缩
- 新增 _select_compression_strategy() 方法

### lobster_grep TF-IDF 重排序（v2.5.0）
- 添加 TFIDFScorer 导入
- lobster_grep() 增加 use_tfidf_rerank 参数
- 结合 FTS5 rank 和 tfidf

fix: add metadata param to base send_image and forward in send_animation

_send_response_parts() calls send_image(metadata=_thread_metadata) but
the base class signature didn't accept metadata, crashing platforms that
don't override send_image. send_animation already had the param but
wasn't forwarding it.

fix: exclude hidden directories from find/grep search backends (#1558)

The primary injection vector in #1558 was search_files discovering
catalog cache files in .hub/index-cache/ via find or grep, which
don't skip hidden directories like ripgrep does by default.

Three-layer fix:

1. _search_files (find): add -not -path '*/.*' to exclude hidden
   directories, matching ripgrep's default behavior.

2. _search_with_grep: add --exclude-dir='.*' to skip hidden
   directories in the grep fallback pa

fix: add --yes flag to bypass confirmation in /skills install and uninstall (#1647)

Fixes hanging when using /skills install or /skills uninstall from the
TUI — bare input() calls hang inside prompt_toolkit's event loop.

Changes:
- Add skip_confirm parameter to do_install() and do_uninstall()
- Separate --yes/-y (confirmation bypass) from --force (scan override)
  in both argparse and slash command handlers
- Update usage hint for /skills uninstall to show [--yes]

fix: use tar-fs for archive creation to fix Windows MAX_PATH issue

Windows bsdtar silently skips files with paths >= 260 characters
(libarchive bug), causing the runtime archive to be 56MB instead of
700MB+. node_modules paths routinely exceed 260 chars.

Replace system tar with tar-fs (pure JS streaming tar implementation):
- No MAX_PATH limitation (uses Node.js fs which supports long paths)
- Works identically on Windows/macOS/Linux
- Produces standard POSIX tar with pax headers for long file

fix: prevent infinite 400 loop on context overflow + block prompt injection via cache files (#1630, #1558)

* fix: prevent infinite 400 failure loop on context overflow (#1630)

When a gateway session exceeds the model's context window, Anthropic may
return a generic 400 invalid_request_error with just 'Error' as the
message.  This bypassed the phrase-based context-length detection,
causing the agent to treat it as a non-retryable client error.  Worse,
the failed user message was still persisted

feat: auto-detect local file paths in gateway responses for native media delivery (#1640)

Small models (7B-14B) can't reliably use MEDIA: or IMAGE: syntax. This
adds extract_local_files() to BasePlatformAdapter that regex-detects
bare local file paths ending in image/video extensions, validates them
with os.path.isfile(), and delivers them as native platform attachments.

feat: standalone anti-compaction — no Lossless Claw needed

Removed LCM dependency entirely. The session-journal hook now provides
complete 3-layer anti-compaction protection on its own:

1. MESSAGE CAPTURE — every message logged to .session_journal.jsonl
2. PERIODIC SUMMARY — every 10 msgs, external LLM → .session_snapshot.md
   + CURRENT.md updated every 5 msgs (no LLM, lightweight)
3. COMPACT:BEFORE — hook listens to session:compact:before event,
   forces immediate summary + CURRENT.md save

fix(tools): remove unnecessary crontab requirement from cronjob tool (#1638)

* fix(tools): remove unnecessary crontab requirement from cronjob tool

The hermes cron system is internal — it uses a JSON-based scheduler
ticked by the gateway (cron/scheduler.py), not system crontab.

The check for shutil.which('crontab') was preventing the cronjob tool
from being available in environments without crontab installed (e.g.
minimal Ubuntu containers).

- Remove shutil.which('crontab') check fr

guardrail compliance.

fix: eliminate cron subprocess storm — background warmer only, never blocks hot path

Root cause of persistent /api/agents hangs: getCronAgentActivity() enqueued
subprocess calls on every cache miss, creating a constant storm of
'openclaw cron runs' processes that starved the event loop.

Fix: cron data now populated ONLY by background _warmCronCacheOnce() which
runs once at startup (5s delay) then every 10 min. The hot path
(getCronAgentActivity) returns cached data or null — never spawns.

   false-splitting values that contain uppercase text.

   never get updated an

Fix pending zip cleanup to prevent infinite retry loops

When zip generation fails (e.g., no files fetched from GitHub), the
crawler was not properly cleaning up the failed zip from the pending
list. This caused the crawler to re-attempt generating the same failing
zips on every subsequent run.

Changes:
- Remove pending zip when generation succeeds or when manifest/URL is invalid
- Check if zip file exists on disk before retry; if missing/too small,
  remove from pending to avoid infinite loops

getClient() calls had no timeout protection - if the HTTP calls hang
(e.g. during initialization or after gateway restart), the entire agent
pipeline blocks indefinitely with no timeout, no error, and no fallback.

This wraps the auto-recall block with the existing withTi

feat(capture): replace blind truncation with content-aware compression

Messages exceeding the 10k char API limit were sliced mid-content,
producing garbled JSON, logs, and code that the backend couldn't
extract facts from. The new compressor detects the content format
(JSON, YAML, logs, code, config, or plain text) and produces a
structured summary preserving key metadata plus head/tail of the
original content within the size budget.

fix: enable Pipeline Report + Intelligence + Build Update scheduled tweets

with ONE_HOUR delay means scheduled checks only fire 1 hour AFTER
startup. Since the bot rarely survives a full hour, Pipeline Report,
Intelligence Thread, and Build Update never fired.

checkScheduledTweets() function that runs immediately on startup and
repeats every 15 minutes (CH

Update README.md

server-side authoritative risk scoring in guard engine

Agents can no longer bypass risk_threshold policies by sending
risk_score: 0. The guard now computes a server-side score from
action_type, reversible, systems_touched, and declared_goal, then
uses max(computed, agent-reported) for policy evaluation. Response
returns both risk_score (authoritative) and agent_risk_score (raw).

the entire container root to the remote, deleting everything on the
remote that doesn't exist locally. Now throws a clear error if
localPath is absolute.

fix(security): allow combined short flags in allowedFlags validation

Combined short flags like `sort -rn` were rejected even though `-r` and
`-n` are both individually whitelisted. The validator now decomposes
all-letter combined flags and checks each character against the
allowedFlags list. Partially-allowed combinations (e.g. `-ro` where `-o`
is not whitelisted) are still correctly blocked.

fix: align plugin ID with package name to prevent session bloat (#52)

The manifest and plugin entry declared id as "hxa-connect" but OpenClaw's
plugin loader infers "openclaw-hxa-connect" from the package name. This
mismatch caused repeated re-initialization and session bloat.

Channel name "hxa-connect" remains unchanged — only the plugin ID is updated.

fix(vlm): strip <think> reasoning tags from model responses before storage (#690)

Reasoning-capable LLMs (MiniMax-M2.5, DeepSeek-R1, QwQ) served via vLLM
embed <think>...</think> blocks in message.content. These were stored
verbatim in file summaries and directory overviews, polluting semantic
search and wasting token budget during context loading.

Add _clean_response() in VLMBase that strips <think> tags, and call it
in all three backends (OpenAI, VolcEngine, LiteLLM) at every return point.

feat(user-message): remove 'you' in user message

fix: cron cache starving event loop — CRON_CACHE_TTL 30s→5min, subprocess timeout 15s→6s

Root cause: openclaw cron runs subprocess (one per cron agent) with 30s cache TTL + 15s timeout
was constantly re-spawning, blocking the event loop. /api/agents hung indefinitely.

- CRON_CACHE_TTL: 30s → 300s (5 min)
- AGENTS_CACHE_TTL: 30s → 60s
- Cron subprocess timeout: 15s → 6s (--timeout 4000)
- Queue delay between jobs: 500ms → 2000ms
- SSE warm interval: 30s → 60s
- All cache warmers in setImmediate

feat: initial sense-memory SDK — sovereign persistence for OpenClaw agents

Encrypted key-value memories and journal entries on Nostr relays.
The agent writes to itself using its own NostrKey identity — nobody
else can read them.

- Key-value: NIP-78 replaceable events (remember/recall/forget)
- Journal: NIP-04 DMs to self (append-only stream)
- All content NIP-44 encrypted
- Key validation: no slashes, backslash, null bytes, path traversal
- Content capped at 65000 chars, queries capped at 1000

fix(aur): extract binary from AppImage instead of installing it directly

The AppImage bundles Ubuntu 22.04 WebKitGTK and Mesa/EGL libraries which
conflict with Arch Linux system GPU drivers, causing EGL_BAD_ALLOC errors
and broken rendering. Extract the native binary from the AppImage during
prepare() and install it directly so it uses system WebKitGTK instead.

fix(aur): prevent makepkg from stripping AppImage binary

AppImages are ELF stubs with a squashfs filesystem appended. makepkg's
default strip step removes everything after the ELF sections, destroying
the payload and reducing the ~96MB AppImage to ~0.9MB. Add options=('!strip')
to preserve the full AppImage.

fix: 降级 transformers 到 4.48.0 以解决与 lerobot 的依赖冲突

问题：
- transformers 5.3.0 要求 huggingface-hub >=1.3.0
- lerobot 0.4.x 要求 huggingface-hub <0.36.0
- 两者完全 incompatible，没有交集

解决方案：
- 降级 transformers 从 5.3.0 → 4.48.0
- transformers 4.48.0 要求 huggingface-hub <1.0,>=0.24.0
- huggingface-hub 0.35.2 同时满足两者要求

兼容版本组合：
- lerobot 0.4.4
- transformers 4.48.0
- huggingface-hub 0.35.2
- torch 2.5.1
- torchvision 0.20.1

fix: retry resolve on current conn after reconnect

If resolveApproval fails on the original connection (e.g. a reconnect
happened during a slow human-review escalation), retry on the current
active connection. Extracts sendResolve() helper for the write logic.

Prevents in-flight escalation approvals from being silently lost when
a reconnect occurs between escalation start and human decision.

fix: skip stale cron jobs on gateway restart instead of firing immediately

When the gateway restarts after being down past a scheduled run time,
recurring jobs (cron/interval) were firing immediately because their
next_run_at was in the past. Now jobs more than 2 minutes late are
fast-forwarded to the next future occurrence instead.

- get_due_jobs() checks staleness for cron/interval jobs
- Stale jobs get next_run_at recomputed and saved
- Jobs within 2 minutes of their schedule still fire nor

fix: 触发检测移到脚本开头，实现真正实时上报

修复根本问题：触发检测原来在脚本末尾（数据已采集完才检测），
导致 trigger.json 只能在下一次 cron 运行时才被处理，且处理后
也没有重新采集数据，实时触发完全无效。

新逻辑：
- cron 固定每分钟运行
- 脚本开头获取 Token，立即检测 trigger.json
- 无触发且未到定时周期（默认15分钟）→ 快速退出（< 2s）
- 有触发或到定时周期 → 完整采集上报
- Token 复用，避免重复请求

通勤高频：通勤中将 LAST_REPORT_FILE 时间戳回拨14分钟，
使每分钟都满足"超过15分钟"的条件，实现每分钟上报，
无需修改 cron 配置。

docs: 更新 README 与 .gitignore，完善免编译安装与架构说明

## 修改概览

### .gitignore
- 新增忽略项 `skill`，避免本地 skill 目录被提交
- 文件末尾补全换行符

### README.md

#### 项目描述与目录
- 明确目标设备为 RayNeo X3 Pro，简介中补充「阿里云 DashScope Paraformer」「OpenClaw 智能体网关」「Markdown 渲染」等关键词
- 目录结构调整：原「快速开始」拆分为「下载与安装（免编译）」与「从源码构建（开发者）」，新增「依赖说明」入口

#### 功能与手势
- 功能表：补充「眼镜内设置」（三击镜腿打开设置页），语音识别改为「WebSocket 全双工」，Markdown 注明「CommonMark + GFM 表格」，多语言 ASR 注明「共 8 种语言」
- 手势表：改为「主界面动作 / 设置页动作」双列；三击改为「打开设置页」；滑动在主界面为「AI 回复滚动」、在设置页为「切换选项」；增加长按与双指点击的注意事项

#### 技术架构
- 技术架构由

fix: send_animation metadata, MarkdownV2 inline code splitting, tirith cosign-free install (#1626)

* fix: Anthropic OAuth compatibility — Claude Code identity fingerprinting

Anthropic routes OAuth/subscription requests based on Claude Code's
identity markers. Without them, requests get intermittent 500 errors
(~25% failure rate observed). This matches what pi-ai (clawdbot) and
OpenCode both implement for OAuth compatibility.

feat: add task queue management API (#395)

Add CRUD endpoints for task queue management:
- GET /api/tasks/status — summary counts by status
- GET /api/tasks/list — list tasks with optional ?status= filter
- DELETE /api/tasks/{id} — remove a task
- POST /api/tasks/{id}/retry — re-queue a completed/failed task

Uses /api/tasks/* namespace to avoid conflict with existing
/api/queue/status (command lane occupancy) endpoint.

task_retry only allows resetting 'completed' or 'failed' tasks —

* fix(updater): use explicit branch ref to avoid tracking config issues

git pull --ff-only fails when local branch tracks a deleted remote
branch. Use git fetch origin main:main instead, which always updates
local main regardless of current branch or tracking configuration.

- Two-tier identity: 28 flavor canonical + per-user wallets
- Path traversal

fix(memory-openviking): share pending clientPromise across dual-context registrations (#681)

OpenClaw loads the plugin in two separate contexts ([gateway] and [plugins]),
each calling register() and creating its own pending clientPromise. However,
start() only runs in one context, leaving the other context's promise permanently
pending — causing the before_agent_start hook to hang forever on await getClient().

Fix by introducing a module-level localClientPendingPromises map in client.ts.

linear dotProductSimilarity scan) with a single vectorDb.search(vec, 1,

instead of O(f*m) and eliminates ~50 unnecessary embedding API calls per run.

fix(ci): compute AUR checksum without updpkgsums to avoid blob size limit

updpkgsums downloads all sources (including the 100MB AppImage) inside
the AUR action container, causing the downloaded file to be committed
and exceeding AUR's 250 KiB blob limit. Pre-compute the SHA256 in the
runner and patch it into PKGBUILD directly.

fix(ios-sandbox): fix three root causes of empty stdout

Diagnosed by running the WASM with wasmtime on macOS:

1. argv = ["/bin/sh"] (was ["tinyemu"])
   container2wasm's init reads WASM argv[0] as the container CMD to exec.
   With "tinyemu" it was trying exec: "2" (embedded Docker CMD was missing/
   corrupted), so /bin/sh never started → no output.

2. Keep inPipe[1] open until after wasm_runtime_deinstantiate
   TinyEMU's serial console is a real Linux tty.  Closing the write-end of
   stdi

fix: clear diagnosed set when peer reaches dead — prevent endless growth

diagnosed_peers.json grew endlessly because evicted peers never return
to alive_peers(), so the recovery path never cleared them. After weeks,
ALL peers would be in diagnosed and the steward stops diagnosing.

Fix: remove dead/evicted peers from diagnosed after escalation task is
created. If peer comes back and goes suspect again, it gets re-diagnosed.

fix: relax timing thresholds in policy adversarial tests (100ms -> 500ms) (#1294)

These tests guard against catastrophic regex backtracking (seconds/minutes),
not 12ms differences. CI runners with coverage instrumentation (cargo-llvm-cov)
consistently exceed the 100ms threshold due to overhead, causing flaky failures.
500ms still catches real regressions while tolerating CI variability.

[skip-regression-check]

Replaces the old dead-peer-only task creation with a full Call and Response loop:

   Send diagnostic_report via NADI to the peer.

2. RESPONSE: Next cycle, check if peer recovered (back to ALIVE).
   If yes: loop closed, remove from diagnosed set. Peer was just a C

fix(cron): use average interval and add tool-side pre-validation

Two fixes:

1. Use average interval instead of minimum gap for rate limiting.
   */16 produces gaps of 16-16-16-12 at hour boundaries — minimum
   is 12 min but average is 15 min, which better reflects intent.

2. Add cron expression and interval pre-validation in manage_schedule
   tool so the model sees errors before the frontend RPC call.
   Previously the tool always returned "success" and the model
   hallucinated successful

fix: chat

fix(ios-sandbox): pass explicit pipe fds to set_wasi_args_ex; add thread env init

Three fixes combined:
1. Pass inPipe[0]/outPipe[1]/errPipe[1] directly to wasm_runtime_set_wasi_args_ex
   instead of -1/-1/-1.  WAMR stores/dups these into its internal WASI fd table,
   so TinyEMU fd_write(1,…) calls land in our pipe.  Passing -1 had undefined
   semantics — WAMR may have treated it as an invalid fd and silently dropped writes,
   explaining the persistent exec_ok=true / stdout_bytes=0 symptom.

fix: SQLite 'database is locked' by adding busy timeout (#6474)

The async engine is created without a busy timeout, so concurrent
writes (agent responses, metrics, session updates) fail instantly
with 'database is locked' instead of waiting for the lock.

Add connect_args={'timeout': 30} for SQLite engines so the driver
waits up to 30 seconds for the write lock. Combined with the existing
WAL journal mode, this handles the typical concurrent write bursts
from agent + metrics + session operation

feat: migrate XMTP from node-sdk to CLI subprocess wrapper

Replace @xmtp/node-sdk (native GLIBC 2.38 bindings) with @xmtp/cli
subprocess wrapper. Fixes XMTP on Debian 12, Ubuntu 22.04, and
OpenClaw sandboxes.

- Rewrite cli/src/lib/xmtp.ts to shell out to xmtp binary
- Auto-sync private key from ~/.sherwood/config.json → ~/.xmtp/.env
- Map --testnet to XMTP --env dev, mainnet to --env production
- Reactions/markdown now sent as ChatEnvelope text (no native types)
- Remove native binding externa

fix(dashboard): portable gateway status detection

Replace lsof-only port check with a fallback chain:
1. lsof (macOS/Linux with lsof installed)
2. bash /dev/tcp (Linux without lsof)
3. curl (universal HTTP fallback)

This makes gateway status detection work across different
environments where lsof may not be available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix workspace_id UUID format mismatch in Notion credential lookup

The tracking URL decodes workspace_id without dashes (e.g., 2be6a52cd8a0812a86840003b0ffbf46)
but MongoDB stores it with dashes (e.g., 2be6a52c-d8a0-812a-8684-0003b0ffbf46).

This caused workspace_id lookups to always fail, falling back to any available credential.

Changes:
- Add normalize_workspace_id() to convert UUIDs to canonical format
- Try multiple formats in get_credential_by_workspace() for robustness

fix(gateway): drop events for unknown local tasks (#73)

Gateway broadcasts all session events without filtering. When multiple
users share one Gateway, the client receives messages for sessions it
never created, causing SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_FOREIGNKEY errors on persist
and potential cross-user session leakage.

Add a local task existence check in both handleChatEvent and
handleAgentEvent before processing any gateway event.

fix(instance): 修复实例状态恢复逻辑，支持 error → active 转换

问题：
- RemoteInstanceService.processHeartbeat() 只处理 offline → active 的恢复
- 当实例因 WebSocket 超时被标记为 error 状态后，即使 HTTP 心跳正常也无法恢复
- 导致实例 status='error', health_status='healthy' 的不一致状态

修复：
- 扩展状态恢复逻辑，同时处理 offline 和 error 状态
- 当实例状态为 offline 或 error 且收到健康心跳时，自动恢复为 active
- 改进日志记录，显示恢复前的状态

相关文件：
- platform/backend/src/services/RemoteInstanceService.ts:233

fix(ios-sandbox): fix stdout deadlock — deinstantiate before drainGroup.wait()

Root cause of empty stdout on all commands:

drainGroup.wait() blocks until drain threads see EOF on outPipe[0].
EOF requires ALL write ends closed:
  - our copy: outPipe[1] (kept open while VM runs)
  - WAMR's dup'd copy: closed only by wasm_runtime_deinstantiate()

With defer{deinstantiate} at scope exit (= after drainGroup.wait()),
drain waits forever for EOF that only arrives post-deinstantiate.

feat: OpenClaw native bridge — auto-start in rampart serve (v0.9.5)

rampart serve now auto-starts an OpenClaw gateway bridge when
~/.openclaw/openclaw.json is found. The bridge subscribes to the
OpenClaw operator.approvals WebSocket scope and handles exec approvals:

- Hard deny (rm -rf /, credential access, etc) → auto-resolved
  immediately, never shown to user
- Safe commands → auto-resolved allow-once, no prompt
- Human-review commands (action: ask/require_approval) → escalated
  to Rampart

fix(docker): merge common-env anchor in hotplex-base service

Docker Compose extends mechanism overwrites (not merges) environment
sections when child service defines its own. Use YAML anchor merge
syntax (<<: *common-env) to properly inherit environment variables
from common-env anchor.

This fixes:
- SQLite "out of memory (14)" error (HOTPLEX_MESSAGE_STORE_SQLITE_PATH now set)
- "mkdir /hotplex: permission denied" error (HOTPLEX_PROJECTS_DIR now set)

archive creation and hydration extraction.

- runtime-hydrator: tar.extract() replaces spawn("tar")
- Both are now cross-platform consistent (no system tar

* resolve 7 open issues — race condition, NPE, file delete, auto-save ([#250](https://github.com/HankHuang0516/EClaw/issues/250)) ([e076e04](https://github.com/HankHuang0516/EClaw/commit/e076e041a8330ce67c4b270a1b43df18d880bd38))

fix: use lazy import for lark-oapi to prevent startup crash when package is missing

Users upgrading via docker cp without rebuilding the Docker image would
encounter ModuleNotFoundError for lark_oapi, crashing the entire backend.

This follows the same pattern already used by dingtalk_stream.py and
wecom_stream.py - wrapping the SDK import in try/except ImportError
and gracefully disabling the feature when not available.

fix: use Node.js for SDK subprocess on Windows and capture stderr

On Windows, cli.js (a Node.js program) fails when run with Bun due to
insufficient Node.js compat. Prefer node.exe on Windows bundled mode with
fallback to Bun. Also capture SDK subprocess stderr for diagnostics,
set HOME from USERPROFILE if missing, and add Windows-specific error
message suggesting Node.js installation.

fix: strip \\?\ path prefix and pass HOME/USERPROFILE env on Windows sidecar

Tauri's resource_dir() returns \\?\C:\... extended-length paths on Windows,
which can break downstream path resolution. Strip the prefix before setting
RESOURCES_DIR. Also pass USERPROFILE and HOME env vars to the sidecar process
since cli.js needs them and they may not be inherited in bundled mode.

fix(ios-sandbox): keep pipe fds open until after wasm_runtime_instantiate

WAMR stores the stdinfd/stdoutfd/stderrfd from set_wasi_args_ex and
calls dup() on them during wasm_runtime_instantiate to create its
internal copies. Closing the originals before instantiate → EBADF
→ "Init wasi environment failed".

Fix: close inPipe[0], outPipe[1], errPipe[1] immediately after
instantiate returns (WAMR has its own dup'd copies by then).

- QR 码轮询逻辑复杂且用户体验不佳

### 新的流程
- OAuth 回调成功后检查实例状态
  - 如果已有实例 → 跳转到聊天页面
  - 如果无实例 → 跳转到实例列表页面（/instances?tab=unclaimed）

### 代码简化
- 移除 100+ 行 QR 码相关代码
- 移除不必要的依赖和状态管理
- 统一使用 AuthContext 管理认证状态

- 仅影响 OAuth 回调后的用户体验
- 不影响 QR 码生成 API 和其他认证流程

fix: 修复远程实例通信问题

## 问题背景
1. 云服务商安全组阻止端口 3000-3002，仅开放 80/443
2. Agent WebSocket 连接频繁超时断开（每 30 秒）
3. 实例状态被标记为 offline 后无法恢复为 active
4. 导致自动认领功能无法工作（要求 status = 'active'）

## 修复内容

### 1. 修复部署脚本 WebSocket URL
**文件**: platform/backend/scripts/deploy-remote-agent.sh

- 修改前: ws://118.25.0.190:3002/?api_key=...
- 修改后: ws://118.25.0.190/ws?api_key=...
- 原因: 通过 nginx 端口 80 的 /ws 路径代理，避免直接连接被阻止的端口

### 2. 修复实例状态恢复逻辑
**文件**: platform/backend/src/services/RemoteInstanceService.ts

- 添加: heartbeat 处理时检查实例状态

wasm_runtime_set_wasi_args_ex API, which accepts explicit

time via its own dup() — even redirecting before instantiate was
unreliable due to WAMR's internal fd management.

wasm_runtime_set_wasi_args_ex bypasses this entirely by passing our
pipe fds directly

fix(ios-sandbox): move fd redirects before wasm_runtime_instantiate

WAMR captures dup() of STDIN/STDOUT/STDERR at instantiation time to
build its internal WASI fd table. Any dup2 redirect after instantiate
has no effect — output always went to the original process fds.

Fix: redirect all pipes (stdin, stdout, stderr) BEFORE calling
wasm_runtime_set_wasi_args + wasm_runtime_instantiate. This makes
WAMR's WASI fd table point to our pipes from the start.

fix: use execFileSync for tar archive to avoid cmd.exe path mangling

On Windows, execSync runs through cmd.exe which can mangle paths and
glob patterns, causing tar to silently archive only a subset of files
(56MB instead of 700MB+). Switch to execFileSync which bypasses the
shell entirely.

ci(ios-sandbox): skip wizer (incompatible with c2w TinyEMU output)

container2wasm's TinyEMU Wasm does not export 'wizer.initialize',
so wizer pre-boot checkpointing is not supported. Ship the raw c2w
output directly. Cold boot adds ~15-60s per sandbox_exec call but
the sandbox is otherwise fully functional.

perf: add proper cache headers — no-cache for index.html, immutable for hashed assets

Standard SPA caching best practice: index.html is never cached so
browsers always fetch the latest version on refresh. Hashed assets
(JS/CSS with Vite content hashes) are cached for 1 year since their
filenames change on every build. This eliminates the need for users
to manually clear browser cache after deployments.

fix: add Voyage embedding provider compatibility and address review feedback (#216)

Voyage AI (and Voyage-style proxies) expose an OpenAI-compatible
embeddings endpoint but reject standard OpenAI request fields and use
different field names for task hints and output dimensions.

Without handling these differences, all embedding calls to Voyage
endpoints failed with 400 Bad Request: Unknown request body key:
encoding_format, and task/dimension configuration was silently dropped.

fix: make sidecar PATH setup cross-platform for Windows support

The spawn_sidecar() function used Unix-only assumptions: HOME env var,
colon PATH separator, /opt/homebrew paths, and Unix nvm resolution.
On Windows this corrupted PATH (C: interpreted as separator) and failed
to find Node.js/Bun. Now uses USERPROFILE fallback, platform-correct
PATH separator, Windows-specific tool paths (scoop, nvm-windows), and
correct RESOURCES_DIR resolution for Windows exe layout.

base64url-encoded, zlib-compressed payloads with metadata including:
- space_id (workspace_id) - can be used for exact credential lookup
- l (actual page URL) - the real notion.so page URL

This change:
- Adds decode_tracking_url_workspace_id() to extract space_id from tracking URLs
- Adds decode_tracking_url_page_url() to get the actual page URL
- Updates NotionEmailNoti

feat(ios): add Alpine 3.21 sandbox via TinyEMU RISC-V + WAMR

Implements the ai.flutterclaw/sandbox MethodChannel on iOS using:
- TinyEMU (MIT) — RISC-V64 CPU emulator compiled to Wasm
- WAMR (Apache 2.0) — interpreter-mode Wasm runtime, no JIT, App Store safe
- Alpine 3.21 userspace (pre-installed: python3, pip, git, curl, wget, jq, bash)

Key design decisions:
- No internet in guest VM (WAMR networking WIP); packages pre-bundled at build time
- /root/ persistence via WAMR --dir + container2was

fix: rewrite chat commands with manual dispatch for Commander compatibility

Commander can't dispatch subcommands when the parent command has a
positional <name> arg — it always runs the parent action. All chat
subcommands (send, log, members, add, react) were dead code.

Rewrites chat as `chat <name> [action] [actionArgs...]` with a single
.action() that manually routes based on the action argument. Also moves
`syndicate init-chat` into `chat <name> init` where it belongs.

fix(db): force UTC timezone for mysql2 connections to prevent query offset

When MySQL server uses a non-UTC timezone (e.g. UTC+8) but Node.js runs
in UTC, mysql2 serializes Date objects as UTC strings while MySQL interprets
them in its session timezone. This causes an 8-hour offset in timestamp
comparisons, making recent audit records invisible.

- Set timezone: '+00:00' on mysql2 pool for client-side Date serialization
- SET time_zone = '+00:00' on each connection for MySQL session consistency

Merge pull request #25 from imthatcarlos/fix/ens-text-record-via-vault

fix: route ENS text record writes through vault executeBatch

fix: route ENS text record writes through vault executeBatch

The vault owns the ENS subdomain node, so only the vault can call
L2Registry.setText. Previously the CLI called L2Registry directly from
the creator's EOA which reverted with Unauthorized.

Now setTextRecord routes through vault.executeBatch → delegatecall →
executor lib → L2Registry.setText, so L2Registry sees msg.sender = vault.
Also adds L2Registry to allowed targets (idempotent) and waits for that
tx to mine before firing executeB

fix(tool): expand cron_add and cron_update parameter schemas (#3671)

The schedule field in cron_add used a bare {"type":"object"} with a
description string encoding a tagged union in pseudo-notation. The patch
field in cron_update was an opaque {"type":"object"} despite CronJobPatch
having nine fully-typed fields. Both gaps cause weaker instruction-following
models to produce malformed or missing nested JSON when invoking these tools.

Changes:
- cron_add: expand schedule into a oneOf discrimin

Fix crash when sending messages while Pi is busy

When a message arrives while Pi is already processing, send it
with streamingBehavior: "followUp" so Pi queues it instead of
rejecting with "Agent is already processing".

If a prompt errors or times out anyway, kill the Pi process so
the next message gets a clean subprocess (instead of hitting a
stale busy process).

fix: context counter shows cached token count in status bar

Anthropic prompt caching splits input into cache_read_input_tokens,
cache_creation_input_tokens, and non-cached input_tokens. The context
counter only read input_tokens (non-cached portion), showing ~3 tokens
instead of the real ~18K total. Now includes cached portions for
Anthropic native provider only — other providers (OpenAI, OpenRouter,
Codex) already include cached tokens in their prompt_tokens field.

foreach loop for every rule that carries a shell filter.  Moving the
computation before the loop avoids the repeated allocation on the hot
command-evaluation path.

NotificationCategorizer.MatchesRule: the static Regex.IsMatch overload
that accepts a matchTimeout bypasses .NET's internal regex cache, so a
new Regex object was compiled on every incoming

fix(tools) Wire activated toolset into dispatch (#3747)

* fix(tools): wire ActivatedToolSet into tool dispatch and spec advertisement

When deferred MCP tools are activated via tool_search, they are stored
in ActivatedToolSet but never consulted by the tool call loop.
tool_specs is built once before the iteration loop and never refreshed,
so the provider API tools[] parameter never includes activated tools.
find_tool only searches the static registry, so execution dispatch also
fails silently.

fix(integrations): wire Cron and Browser status to config fields (#3750)

Both entries had hardcoded |_| IntegrationStatus::Available, ignoring
the live config entirely. Users with cron.enabled = true or
browser.enabled = true saw 'Available' on the /integrations dashboard
card instead of 'Active'.

Root cause: status_fn closures did not capture the Config argument.

Fix: replace the |_| stubs with |c| closures that check c.cron.enabled
and c.browser.enabled respectively, matching the pattern us
